title: No chat name validation allows creation of chats with empty or oversized names
description: The createPublicChat method (line 127) extracts the chat name with message.string(forField: wired.chat.name) and only checks that the field exists (guard != nil). It does not validate that the name is non-empty after trimming whitespace, nor does it enforce a maximum length. A user can create public chats with empty names, whitespace-only names, or extremely long names (limited only by P7 message size). Similarly, no check prevents duplicate chat names.
recommendation: Validate name: guard !name.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty else { ... }. Add a maximum length check (e.g., 255 characters). Consider checking for duplicate names.
